title: Implement Mermaid bead graph renderer
description: Add `render_bead_graph(beads, config) -> str` in a new graph module, including node label formatting, status-icon mapping, title truncation, Mermaid-safe node identifiers, planned dependency edges, and corrective dotted edges for corrective beads.
acceptance_criteria: A new renderer function returns raw Mermaid starting with `graph TD`.
Node identifiers replace hyphens with underscores while labels preserve the original bead ID.
Node labels follow `<bead_id>: <truncated_title> [<agent_type>] <status_icon>` with titles truncated at 40 characters using `...`.
Status icons are mapped correctly for `done`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `ready`, `open`, and `handed_off`.
Solid `-->` edges are emitted only for dependencies present in the provided bead set.
Corrective beads identified via the configured corrective suffix emit dotted `-.->` edges to their parent bead.
Orphan beads remain as isolated node definitions when no edges apply.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `render_bead_graph(beads, config) -> str` in `src/codex_orchestrator/graph.py`. The renderer emits raw Mermaid starting with `graph TD`, preserves original bead IDs in labels, converts node IDs by replacing hyphens with underscores, truncates titles at 40 characters with `...`, emits solid dependency edges only when both beads are present, and emits dotted corrective edges to in-set parent beads based on the configured corrective suffix. Verified with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/graph.py` and an import check for `codex_orchestrator.graph`.

execution_history.summary: Implemented a new Mermaid bead graph renderer module with node label formatting, status-icon mapping, dependency edges, corrective dotted edges, and Mermaid-safe node identifiers.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
